title: Crispy Algerian Chicken Tagine
description: A savory, highprotein tagine with crisp chicken thighs, aromatic spices and tender rice noodles, simmered to perfection for a solodinner delight.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large, heavy‑bottomed pan or tagine over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Increase the heat to medium‑high, add the chicken pieces skin‑side down, and sear until golden on all sides, about 5–6 minutes.
Sprinkle the cumin and smoked paprika over the chicken, tossing to coat evenly.
Pour in the diced tomatoes and chicken broth, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover, reduce heat to low, and let the tagine cook for 20 minutes, allowing the chicken to become tender and flavors to meld.
Add the rice noodles, stir gently, and cover again for 8–10 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve hot.
